engaged_works.title: The Jargon of Authenticity
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Routed from the 2026-05-10 Tier-1 publication backlog as an on-disk source witness.
engaged_works.summary: This on-disk text is being wired into the Source tab as a citation witness for Theodor W. Adorno, The Jargon of Authenticity. The entry is intentionally minimal: it records the routed file and basic bibliographic shape so the site can surface the source while fuller review waits. A later pass should tighten the title, edition, and passage coverage against the on-disk witness.

core_thesis: Theodor W. Adorno is included here as a comparator for critical theory: philosophy remains necessary, but only as self-critical thought under damaged social conditions. In Negative Dialektik he defines dialectic by the fact that objects do not go up into their concepts; contradiction indexes the untruth of identity, and the task is to reach the conceptless with concepts without making it identical to them ([Negative Dialektik, Einleitung](cite://adorno/theodoradorno-negativedialektik/Einleitung)). The same work links this method to the priority of the object and to a materialist turn away from the primacy of the subject ([Negative Dialektik, Vorrang des Objekts](cite://adorno/theodoradorno-negativedialektik/Vorrang-des-Objekts)). In Dialectic of Enlightenment, with Horkheimer, he argues that enlightenment must reflect on its own regressive moment: myth is already enlightenment, and enlightenment can revert to mythology and domination ([Dialectic of Enlightenment, Preface and Concept](cite://adorno/dialecticofenlightenmenttheodorw-adornomaxhorkheimer/preface-concept)). The culture-industry chapter applies this to standardized mass culture, where technical rationality functions as domination ([Culture Industry](cite://adorno/dialecticofenlightenmenttheodorw-adornomaxhorkheimer/culture-industry)). The Jargon of Authenticity extends the critique to existential language that presents social conformity as inward depth ([Jargon of Authenticity, ch. 5](cite://adorno/adorno-jargon/5)).

cross_engagements.brief: Karl Marx works the same finite cognition axis in the political-social register. Here the operative value is `genealogically-produced-illusion`; Karl Marx lands on that same value.
Akshapada Gautama presses the same method axis in the methodological register, but with the value `formal-proof-or-inference` rather than `dialectical-development`.
Judith Butler presses the same normativity axis in the political-social register, but with the value `disciplinary-power` rather than `class-structured-social-relation`.
G. W. F. Hegel presses the same social formation axis in the political-social register, but with the value `recognitive-institutional` rather than `commodity-fetish-social-form`.
Abhinavagupta works the same motive force axis in the aesthetic register. Here the operative value is `aesthetic-rapture`; Abhinavagupta lands on that same value.

Adi Sankara touches the same soteric end axis, but not in the same register. This section works it as `ethical-vigilance-without-final-fusion` in the ethical register; Adi Sankara approaches it through the soteriological register.
